meta.description: Design system governance rules for @chebert-pd/ui. Encodes design intent — not just what tokens exist, but how they should relate to each other.

foregroundHierarchy.description: Text colors have an emphasis sequence. foreground is full emphasis (any content). muted-foreground is de-emphasized (descriptions, helper text, h3/h4 as subheadings). muted-foreground must never appear on h1 or h2.

tokens.foreground: Full emphasis — headings, body text, labels, data. No restrictions.
tokens.muted-foreground: De-emphasized — descriptions, helper text, placeholders, h3/h4 subheadings.

violations.pattern: muted-foreground on h1 or h2
violations.reason: Top-level headings require full emphasis
violations.fix: Use foreground (text-foreground) for h1 and h2

violations.pattern: primary-foreground outside a primary-colored surface
violations.reason: primary-foreground is designed for contrast against primary backgrounds
violations.fix: Use foreground or muted-foreground for standard surfaces

surfaceHierarchy.description: Backgrounds have a stacking order: background (canvas) → card (elevated) → secondary (nested) → muted (subtle) → accent (hover) → popover (floating).

violations.pattern: bg-accent used on static (non-interactive) content
violations.reason: accent is reserved for hover/active states
violations.fix: Use bg-secondary or bg-muted for static differentiation

borderHierarchy.description: Borders have tiers: border (structural), border-subtle (low-emphasis), input (interactive surfaces), ring (focus).

tokens.border-subtle: Low-emphasis separators inside surfaces. Must be less prominent than border.
tokens.input: Interactive surfaces — form controls, dialogs, any UI where the user makes a decision

violations.pattern: ring token used outside focus/focus-visible state
violations.reason: ring is exclusively for focus indicators
violations.fix: Use border or input for non-focus borders

violations.pattern: Hardcoded border color (oklch, rgba, hex)
violations.reason: All borders must use semantic tokens
violations.fix: Use border, border-subtle, or input tokens

elevationCoherence.description: Shadows map to component scale. Small components use light shadows. Large overlays use heavy shadows.

violations.pattern: elevation-overlay or elevation-popover on a small component (badge, button, input)
violations.reason: Heavy shadows on small elements break visual scale
violations.fix: Use elevation-surface or elevation-floating

violations.pattern: Hardcoded box-shadow value
violations.reason: All shadows must use semantic elevation tokens
violations.fix: Use elevation-surface, elevation-floating, elevation-overlay, or elevation-popover

violations.pattern: Raw shadow primitive (shadow-y1, shadow-y2, shadow-y6, shadow-y16)
violations.reason: Primitives are for token definitions in globals.css only
violations.fix: Use semantic elevation tokens

semanticColorPairing.description: Status colors are 3-token contracts: base (background tint), -border, -foreground. They must be used as complete sets and never mixed across schemes.

tokenRoles.base: Background tint — light in light mode, dark in dark mode. NOT for text or icons.
tokenRoles.-foreground: Text and icon color. This is the visible content color.
tokenRoles.-solid-foreground: Text on solid background (destructive, brand only).

violations.pattern: text-destructive, text-success, text-warning, text-brand, text-review (base token used for text/icons)
violations.reason: The base token is a background tint (e.g., rose-50 in light mode — nearly invisible as text). Use the -foreground variant for content.
violations.fix: Replace text-destructive with text-destructive-foreground, text-success with text-success-foreground, etc.

violations.pattern: Mixing schemes — e.g., bg-success with text-warning-foreground
violations.reason: Schemes are designed as cohesive sets. Cross-scheme pairing breaks visual coherence.
violations.fix: Use tokens from the same scheme (bg-success + border-success-border + text-success-foreground)

violations.pattern: Mixing solid and tinted variants on the same element
violations.reason: Solid variants are inverted (bold bg + light text). Tinted variants are subtle (light bg + bold text). Mixing them produces unreadable contrast.
violations.fix: Use either the tinted set (destructive/destructive-border/destructive-foreground) or the solid set (destructive-solid/destructive-solid-foreground), not both.

typographyConventions.description: The type system uses weight classes (420/520/620/660 for the variable Inter sans, plus 500 for the Wonder display face in the Wyllolabs theme) and scale utilities. Arbitrary weights and sizes break the system.

weights.420: Body text (p, p-lg, p-sm, form-label, form-control)
weights.500: Wonder display face — Wyllolabs theme only. Wonder ships fixed weights (no variable axis), so 500 is the closest available 'medium'. Used inside .dark[data-theme="labs"] by .h1-.h4, .data-*, .labs-display, .labs-eyebrow, .labs-quote.

violations.pattern: font-bold, font-semibold, font-medium, font-light, font-normal
violations.reason: The design system uses numeric weights (420/520/620/660 for Inter sans, 500 for Wonder display in Labs theme), not named weights
violations.fix: Use font-[420], font-[520], font-[620], or font-[660] for Inter; font-[500] for Wonder display in Labs. Or the corresponding utility class (p, label-md, h2, data-md, labs-display, etc.)

violations.pattern: Arbitrary font size (text-[14px], text-[0.875rem])
violations.reason: The type scale is defined by utility classes, not arbitrary values
violations.fix: Use the scale: text-xs, text-sm, text-base, text-lg, text-xl, text-2xl, text-3xl

violations.pattern: uppercase on text, or uppercase combined with tracking-wide/wider/widest
violations.reason: The design system uses sentence case throughout. All-caps text reduces readability and isn't part of the type system — hierarchy comes from weight (.label-md, .label-sm) and color (text-muted-foreground), not casing. Solo tracking-* is permitted (e.g. letter-spacing on keyboard shortcut hints); only the all-caps + wide-tracking combination is the anti-pattern.
violations.fix: Remove 'uppercase' (and any paired tracking-wide* class). If casing is genuinely required for legal text, brand wordmarks, or initialisms, suppress per line with: // audit-governance: disable-next-line TY-003

iconography.description: Icons use lucide-react exclusively. Each icon has a single semantic role. Arrows are for navigation. Chevrons are for reveal/state. Never mix these roles.

navigation.ArrowUpRight: External links — any link that leaves the portal
navigation.ArrowLeft: Back navigation — return to previous view
navigation.ArrowRight: Forward navigation — proceed to next step or view

reveal.ChevronDown: Expand downward — accordions, dropdowns, select triggers, sort descending, trend down
reveal.ChevronUp: Collapse upward — accordions closed-to-open, sort ascending, trend up
reveal.ChevronRight: Open rightward — side panel triggers, drill-in, expandable rows
reveal.ChevronLeft: Navigate backward in sequence — calendar month navigation, breadcrumb

action.Plus: Add or create a new item
action.X: Dismiss or close — dialogs, sheets, panels, tags, toasts
action.Check: Confirm or selected state — checkboxes, select items, success

utility.MoreHorizontal: Overflow menu trigger — always horizontal, never vertical
utility.GripVertical: Drag handle for resizable or sortable elements

sizing.default: size-4 (16px) — standard inline icons in buttons, inputs, menu items
sizing.small: size-3.5 (14px) — compact contexts like trend indicators, badges
sizing.large: size-5 (20px) — command palette items, prominent standalone icons

violations.pattern: ArrowUpRight or ArrowDownRight used for non-navigation (e.g., trend indicators)
violations.reason: Arrow icons are reserved for navigation. Trends use chevrons.
violations.fix: Use ChevronUp/ChevronDown for trend indicators

violations.pattern: EllipsisVertical or MoreVertical used for overflow menus
violations.reason: Overflow menus always use horizontal dots for consistency
violations.fix: Use MoreHorizontal

violations.pattern: Trash2 used instead of Trash
violations.reason: The design system uses the Trash icon (no line), not Trash2
violations.fix: Use Trash from lucide-react

violations.pattern: Icon-only button without iconOnly prop and aria-label
violations.reason: Icon-only buttons must be square and accessible
violations.fix: Add iconOnly prop to Button and provide an aria-label

violations.pattern: Icon from a library other than lucide-react
violations.reason: All icons must come from lucide-react for visual consistency
violations.fix: Find the equivalent icon in lucide-react or request a custom addition

violations.pattern: Chevron used for navigation or arrow used for reveal/state
violations.reason: Arrows = navigation, chevrons = reveal/state. Do not mix these roles.
violations.fix: Use ArrowLeft/ArrowRight/ArrowUpRight for navigation; ChevronDown/ChevronUp/ChevronRight for reveal

primitiveLeakage.description: Raw primitive tokens (gray-*, violet-*, orange-*) and hardcoded color values should never appear in component source files. Primitives are for token definitions in globals.css only.

violations.pattern: Raw primitive token in component file (gray-*, violet-*, orange-*)
violations.reason: Components must use semantic tokens. If no semantic token exists, flag it as a gap.
violations.fix: Replace with the appropriate semantic token (foreground, muted-foreground, background, etc.)

violations.pattern: Hardcoded color value (#hex, rgb(), rgba(), oklch(), hsl())
violations.reason: All colors must come from the token system
violations.fix: Use a semantic color token or add a new semantic token if one doesn't exist

violations.pattern: Tailwind palette class (text-blue-500, bg-red-400, border-gray-200, etc.)
violations.reason: Standard Tailwind palette classes bypass the design system
violations.fix: Use semantic tokens from the design system
